gloss.en: in the direction of someone or something

words.example: She walked toward the door.

gloss.en: in the direction of someone or something

words.example: They were heading towards the border.

gloss.en: cloth used for drying your body

words.example: Help yourself to a clean towel.

gloss.en: a tall narrow building or part of one

words.example: We climbed the old bell tower.

gloss.en: an object children play with

words.example: He got a toy car for his birthday.

gloss.en: a custom that has existed a long time

words.example: Eating dumplings is a family tradition.

gloss.en: following old customs or ways

words.example: They wore traditional dress at the festival.

gloss.en: the vehicles on a road at a time

words.example: There's always a lot of traffic at this time of day.

gloss.en: connected cars pulled along a railway

words.example: We need to get on the train now.

gloss.en: learning the skills needed for a job

words.example: The new staff start their training tomorrow.

gloss.en: an electric vehicle that runs on street rails

words.example: We took the tram into the city center.

gloss.en: the act of going from place to place

words.example: Air travel has become much cheaper.

gloss.en: a person who travels

words.example: The traveler waited for the next bus.

gloss.en: a person who travels often

words.example: She is a frequent traveller to Belgium.

gloss.en: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads

words.example: My father is a truck driver.

gloss.en: two times; on two occasions

words.example: I've only met him twice.

gloss.en: not sure or not known

words.example: The future of the company is uncertain.

gloss.en: to know the meaning of something

words.example: Can you understand French?

gloss.en: below the surface of the water

words.example: They took an underwater photo of the fish.

gloss.en: worried or slightly afraid

words.example: She felt uneasy walking home alone.

gloss.en: so good you will always remember it

words.example: It was an unforgettable trip.

gloss.en: used to say something is sad or unlucky

words.example: Unfortunately, I won't be able to attend the meeting.

gloss.en: not happy; sad

words.example: He looked unhappy after the bad news.

gloss.en: not good for your health

words.example: Eating too much sugar is unhealthy.

gloss.en: special clothes worn by a group

words.example: The hat is part of the school uniform.

gloss.en: not important

words.example: These small details are unimportant.

gloss.en: a single complete thing or part

words.example: Each unit of the book has ten words.

gloss.en: a place for high-level study and degrees

words.example: Is there a university in this town?

gloss.en: different from what is normal

words.example: It's unusual to see snow here.

gloss.en: towards a higher position

words.example: He jumped up from his chair.

gloss.en: the act of using something

words.example: This tool has many uses.

gloss.en: helpful for doing what you want

words.example: This is a very useful tool.

gloss.en: happening or done most of the time

words.example: This is the usual way of doing it.

gloss.en: low land between hills or mountains

words.example: A river flows through the valley.

gloss.en: a word that shows an action

words.example: "Run" is a verb.

gloss.en: a very small town in the country

words.example: We visited towns and villages all over Spain.

gloss.en: a small string instrument played with a bow

words.example: She plays the violin very well.

gloss.en: a person who visits a place or person

words.example: We've got visitors coming this weekend.

gloss.en: all the words a person knows

words.example: Reading helps you learn new vocabulary.

gloss.en: the sound made when speaking or singing

words.example: I could hear voices in the next room.

gloss.en: the activity of going on foot

words.example: Walking is good for your health.

gloss.en: a small case for money and cards

words.example: He keeps his wallet in his pocket.

gloss.en: a long fight between countries or groups

words.example: Many people died in the war.

gloss.en: an act of cleaning with water and soap

words.example: These towels are ready for a wash.

gloss.en: the act of cleaning with water and soap

words.example: She hung the washing out to dry.

gloss.en: the dishes that need to be cleaned

words.example: I'll do the washing-up after dinner.

gloss.en: not physically strong

words.example: She is still weak after her illness.

gloss.en: the system for finding info online

words.example: I like to search the web for recipes.

gloss.en: a set of connected pages on the internet

words.example: For current prices please visit our website.

gloss.en: a marriage ceremony and the party after

words.example: They invited all their friends to the wedding.

gloss.en: any day except Saturday or Sunday

words.example: I go to school on every weekday.

gloss.en: to cry tears

words.example: She began to weep when she heard the news.

gloss.en: how heavy someone or something is

words.example: The box is about ten kilos in weight.

gloss.en: known by many people; famous

words.example: He is a well-known singer.

gloss.en: in or towards the west

words.example: They live on the west coast.

gloss.en: covered with or full of water

words.example: Don't go out in your wet clothes.

gloss.en: a round object that turns to move things

words.example: One wheel of the bike was broken.

gloss.en: a chair with wheels for people who can't walk

words.example: He moves around in a wheelchair.

gloss.en: used to ask who something belongs to

words.example: Whose house is that?

gloss.en: measuring a large distance side to side

words.example: It's a wide, fast-flowing river.

gloss.en: living in nature, not on a farm

words.example: We saw many wild animals in the park.

gloss.en: with a lot of wind

words.example: It was cold and windy outside.

gloss.en: an alcoholic drink made from grapes

words.example: They drank a glass of red wine.

gloss.en: a person or team that wins something

words.example: The winner gets a free trip to Sydney.

gloss.en: a hope that someone will be happy or well

words.example: Give my good wishes to the family.

gloss.en: not having or using something

words.example: They had gone two days without food.

gloss.en: the hard material that trees are made of

words.example: He chopped some wood for the fire.

gloss.en: made of wood

words.example: She put the keys in a wooden box.

gloss.en: feeling unhappy and afraid about something

words.example: Don't look so worried!

gloss.en: used to show surprise or being impressed

words.example: Wow! You look terrific!

gloss.en: the activity of putting words on paper

words.example: He is good at reading and writing.

gloss.en: an informal way of saying yes

words.example: "Are you coming?" "Yeah, sure."

gloss.en: belonging to you

words.example: Is that book yours?

gloss.en: the number 0; nothing

words.example: The temperature fell to zero last night.
